meta.label: SYNTHETIC SCENARIO SUITE  UI/UX STRESS TEST

scenarios.name: Baseline
scenarios.description: No changes. Validate all derived metrics match baseline.
scenarios.decisionBrief: Steady state. LumaLane is marginally profitable at 2.9% operating margin. 55Cr cash provides ample buffer.
uxFriction: Dashboard shows MRR/ARR labels instead of Monthly/Annual Revenue
Runway shows 999.0mo on Simulate page vs Sustainable on Health Check
Burn shows 0 instead of negative burn (surplus)
Cash Balance Critical alert fires despite 55Cr cash (threshold: <$100K)

scenarios.name: Discounting Push (Promo Month)
scenarios.description: Month 2 only: revenue +8% BUT grossMarginPct -4pp due to heavy discounting
scenarios.decisionBrief: One-month promotional discount. Revenue lifts 8% but GM drops 4pp. Net impact: still profitable but margin thin. Risk: discounting becomes habitual.
uxFriction: Cannot model one-month shocks in Predixen  no month-specific override UI
No discount rate lever in sensitivity sliders
NLP simulation does not support time-bounded changes (Month 2 only)
Simulation treated a -4pp GM change as catastrophic (NO-GO, 5.7mo runway)  math error

scenarios.name: Returns Spike (Post-Holiday)
scenarios.description: Months 1-2: return_rate_pct +6pp. Since returns not directly supported, proxy: GM -3pp and otherCosts +0.5 for reverse logistics.
scenarios.decisionBrief: Post-holiday returns spike causes margin compression and reverse logistics costs. Company flips from profitable to slight burn of 0.52Cr/mo. Runway remains comfortable at 105+ months.
uxFriction: No returns rate field or lever anywhere in Predixen
No reverse logistics cost category
Cannot model return_rate_pct  fundamental gap for retail
No way to express restockable vs. write-off return split
Must use proxy (GM reduction + OtherCosts increase) which loses decision context

scenarios.name: Inventory Stockout (Growth Constrained)
scenarios.decisionBrief: Stockouts constrain growth. Revenue drops 10% while fixed costs remain. Company becomes slightly unprofitable. Stockout-driven revenue loss reveals need for inventory management KPIs.
uxFriction: No stockout_rate_pct field
No fill_rate_pct field
No inventory on hand tracking
Cannot express 'revenue loss due to stockouts'  only raw revenue change
No causal chain visualization (stockout  lost sales  revenue drop)

scenarios.name: Supply Cost Inflation
scenarios.description: Months 1-3: GM -3pp due to COGS inflation from supplier price increases
scenarios.decisionBrief: Supplier inflation compresses margins by 3pp. Company breaks roughly even. Critical to renegotiate supplier terms or pass costs to consumers.
uxFriction: Predixen does not explain causal chains (inflation  COGS  GM  burn)
No COGS breakdown by category (raw materials, packaging, freight)
AI briefing doesn't reference retail-specific cost drivers
Simulation overreacts to moderate GM changes

scenarios.name: New Store Expansion (Capex + Ramp)
scenarios.description: Multi-phase: Months 1-2 capex deposits (-6.0 Cr total cash), Months 3-6 payroll +0.4 opex +0.6, Month 6+ revenue +1.5

expected.verdict: Expansion is NPV positive by month 8-10

scenarios.decisionBrief: Two new stores require 6Cr capex upfront, add 1Cr/mo in opex/payroll during ramp, but generate 1.5Cr/mo incremental revenue by month 6. Payback within 6 months of opening.
uxFriction: CRITICAL: Cannot model multi-step, multi-month scenarios in Predixen
No cash flow delta (capex) input  only total cash balance
No phased timeline (Month 1-2: capex, Month 3-6: ramp, Month 6+: steady state)
No capex vs opex distinction
User would need to run 3 separate simulations and mentally stitch them together
No store-level P&L or unit economics for expansion planning

scenarios.name: Marketing Efficiency Improvement
scenarios.description: Month 2 onward: opex -10% (better ad efficiency) AND revenue +3% (improved ROAS)
scenarios.decisionBrief: Marketing optimization reduces ad spend while improving conversion. Monthly surplus nearly triples from 0.64Cr to 1.63Cr. Strong signal to double down on winning channels.
uxFriction: No ROAS metric or ad spend efficiency tracking
Cannot see marketing spend separately from total opex
Scenario stacking behavior untested  unclear if S6 can layer on top of S1/S4/S5
No attribution model (which channel drives the efficiency gain?)

scenarios.name: Mixed Stacked + Reset Test
scenarios.description: Apply S1 + S4 + S5 simultaneously, then Reset to baseline. Verify exact restoration.

changes.note: Combined effect of S1 (promo month 2) + S4 (COGS inflation months 1-3) + S5 (store expansion)

scenarios.decisionBrief: Stress test for scenario stacking and reset. After applying 3 concurrent changes and resetting, all values must return to exact baseline across Dashboard, Health Check, Simulate, and Data Input pages.
uxFriction: Predixen has no explicit scenario stacking mechanism
No 'what changed' log or assumption ledger
Reset button exists on sensitivity sliders but unclear if it resets NLP simulations
No version history or undo stack for baseline changes
Cannot verify cross-page consistency without manually checking each page
